title: Upstart Q2 2024 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Upstart reported Q2 2024 net revenue of $128 million, down 6% year-over-year, with fee revenue of $131 million, down 9% year-over-year. The company reported a GAAP net loss of $54 million and adjusted EBITDA of negative $9 million. Loan transactions reached approximately 144,000, up 31% year-over-year and 21% sequentially, with an average loan size of $7,700. Contribution margin was 58%, flat sequentially and two percentage points above guidance. Loan automation reached 91% fully automated, marking the 8th consecutive quarterly improvement. Cash on hand was $375 million with loans on balance sheet of $686 million, down from $924 million in the prior quarter. Dave Girouard emphasized that 18% of all accuracy gains in the model since inception have been delivered by the ML team in the last 12 months. Model 18 represents a breakthrough by incorporating APR as both input and output, requiring 1 million parallel predictions per applicant. The company now sources well over half of institutional funding from committed capital partnerships rather than at-will agreements. Small-dollar relief loans grew 57% sequentially and reached break-even. Auto underwriting models improved with 33% better roll rates. HELOC instant approval rate reached 42%, up from 36% in Q1. Q3 2024 guidance projected total revenue of approximately $150 million, fee revenue of approximately $155 million, adjusted EBITDA of approximately negative $5 million, and net income of approximately negative $49 million. H2 2024 fee revenue was projected at approximately $320 million with positive adjusted EBITDA expected in Q4. Sanjay Datta noted that the macro no longer appears to be a direct impediment to the business.
